channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Musician Jesse Welles sings about the news, to great fanfare
item.description: Singer-songwriter Jesse Welles has made a name for himself by singing the news. NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Welles about his music and what motivates his creativity.
item.content:encoded: Singer-songwriter Jesse Welles has made a name for himself by singing the news. NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Welles about his music and what motivates his creativity.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Egypt's Grand Museum opens after many delays
item.description: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt. It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and construction.
item.content:encoded: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt. It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and construction.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: A free diver visited the Strait of Hormuz. Here's what he saw
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz is being blockaded. But a couple weeks ago, one small boat visited and found something surprising below the waves.
item.content:encoded: The Strait of Hormuz is being blockaded. But a couple weeks ago, one small boat visited and found something surprising below the waves.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: What's your favorite music biopic? NPR staffers share their thoughts
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Stephen Thompson and Marc Rivers about which movie biopics make the cut, and the ones that don't.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Stephen Thompson and Marc Rivers about which movie biopics make the cut, and the ones that don't.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson

item.title: A new study shows how ad-based technology is used for surveillance
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Ronald Deibert of The Citizen Lab about a new report he published entitled "Uncovering Webloc," which is about how ad-based technology is used to surveil people.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Ronald Deibert of The Citizen Lab about a new report he published entitled "Uncovering Webloc," which is about how ad-based technology is used to surveil people.
item.dc:creator: Tinbete Ermyas

item.title: Details emerge on the suspect in the WHCD shooting
item.description: The suspect of the shooting at Saturday's White House Correspondents Dinner is described as a "very smart" by students he mentored in Southern California.
item.content:encoded: The suspect of the shooting at Saturday's White House Correspondents Dinner is described as a "very smart" by students he mentored in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Steve Futterman

item.title: WHCD shooting investigation underway: Here's what we know
item.description: Hours after a gunman attempted to breach the White House Correspondents' Dinner, details are slowly emerging about who he is.
item.content:encoded: Hours after a gunman attempted to breach the White House Correspondents' Dinner, details are slowly emerging about who he is.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: The US pulls out of peace talks with Iran
item.description: President Trump says he has cancelled the trip of his representatives to the Iran peace talks in Islamabad, shortly after Iran's Foreign Minister left Pakistan.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says he has cancelled the trip of his representatives to the Iran peace talks in Islamabad, shortly after Iran's Foreign Minister left Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Countries gather in Colombia for the first international conference on how to phase out fossil fuels
item.description: More than 50 countries are gathering in Santa Marta, Colombia next week for the first international conference focused on transitioning away from fossil fuels, which are the single biggest driver of climate change.
item.content:encoded: More than 50 countries are gathering in Santa Marta, Colombia next week for the first international conference focused on transitioning away from fossil fuels, which are the single biggest driver of climate change.
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Covering the Artemis II mission
item.description: Covering the Artemis II mission was a dream assignment for one NPR science correspondent.
item.content:encoded: Covering the Artemis II mission was a dream assignment for one NPR science correspondent.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce
